channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sunday Superquiz edition published with access to additional crossword puzzles for premium subscribers
item.description: This article announces the Sunday, 12 July edition of the Superquiz, a regular puzzle feature written by Andrew Brooks and published for readers of The Sydney Morning Herald. Rather than reporting on ...

item.title: DPWH ensures all NCR pumping stations operational amid heavy rains
item.description: Despite heavy rainfall from Typhoon Inday and the southwest monsoon, the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) confirmed that all pumping stations in the National Capital Region (NCR) were fu...

item.title: Bandits abduct corpse and mourners, demand N450 ransom in Kogi
item.description: Bandits ambushed a vehicle carrying the remains of late Deputy Corps Commander Augustine O. Ikwue of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), along with mourners, in Kogi State. The attack occurred on th...

item.title: CBS News Faces Crisis Under Bari Weiss as Ratings and Staff Plunge
item.description: A media expert has criticized CBS News for its declining performance under editor-in-chief Bari Weiss, citing historic low ratings and staff exodus. Oliver Darcy, a former CNN reporter, detailed the n...

item.title: UK Allocates £1.28m for Wildlife Conservation Projects in Kent
item.description: The UK government has awarded a £1.28 million grant to the Kent Wildlife Trust through Natural England's Species Recovery Programme, aimed at bolstering conservation efforts for endangered species suc...

item.title: England Fans Embrace Pint-Tossing Tradition at World Cup Celebrations
item.description: The phenomenon of fan parks has transformed how England supporters celebrate World Cup matches, with spontaneous pint-tossing celebrations becoming a hallmark of the experience. At venues like The Gar...

item.title: Alleged Forged Documents Link Ivanka Trump's Albanian Resort to Cocaine Money Laundering
item.description: A Miami businessman linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner's planned $1.6 billion luxury resort on Albania's Sazan Island is under investigation for allegedly using forged deeds to launder proceeds ...

item.title: Four Men Convicted for Armed Robberies Targeting Teachers in KZN
item.description: Four men have been convicted by the Pinetown Regional Court for a series of armed robberies targeting schools in KwaZulu-Natal, where teachers were robbed of their cellphones and forced to transfer mo...

item.title: Trump's Truth Social Post Sparks Questions About Timing of Claimed Physical Examination
item.description: A Raw Story article examines renewed scrutiny surrounding President Donald Trump's health after a Truth Social post in which he defended his physical and cognitive fitness. The controversy centered on...

item.title: Kaptagat Forest restoration celebrates 10 years with community-led conservation success
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has commended the Kaptagat Integrated Conservation Programme (KICP) for its 10-year success in restoring Kenya's vital forest ecosystem while empowering local communit...

item.title: Phoenix wins first game of Govs' Cup against Magnolia
item.description: The Phoenix Fuel Masters secured a crucial victory in the PBA Govs' Cup by defeating the Magnolia Hotshots in a closely contested match. The game, held on July 11, 2026, marked Phoenix's successful st...

item.title: Jonathan Swan Defends Maggie Haberman After Trump Criticizes Their Book and Threatens Legal Action
item.description: Journalist Jonathan Swan publicly defended his co-author, Maggie Haberman, after President Donald Trump sharply criticized their recently published book, 'Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency...

item.title: Freak gas accident leaves one dead and three injured in South Australia
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in South Australia's Limestone Coast where a freak gas accident resulted in one man's death and three others being found unconscious inside a residential property. Police ha...

item.title: New Study Reveals How Second Pregnancies Alter Brain Structure and Function
item.description: A groundbreaking study published by Amsterdam University Medical Center in Nature Communications has uncovered how subsequent pregnancies uniquely reshape the maternal brain. Researchers tracked 110 w...

item.title: Nova Scotia Woman Diagnosed with Alpha-Gal Syndrome Faces Dietary Restrictions
item.description: A Pictou County woman in Nova Scotia, Melissa Baines, has been diagnosed with alpha-gal syndrome, a rare allergic reaction triggered by lone star tick bites. After experiencing severe symptoms includi...

item.title: Atiku criticizes Tinubu for not addressing nation after Oyo schoolchildren rescue
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar commended the military's successful rescue of abducted schoolchildren from Oyo State, calling it a victory for Nigeria. He criticized President Tinubu for not addr...

item.title: Paris tourist attractions reduce opening hours as extreme heat grips much of France
item.description: Several of Paris's best-known tourist attractions have shortened their opening hours as France faces its third heat wave since May. The Eiffel Tower announced that it would close early over the weeken...

item.title: Meta Discontinues AI Image Feature Amid Privacy Concerns
item.description: Meta Platforms has decided to discontinue its Muse Image AI feature, which allowed users to generate images using public Instagram accounts, just days after its launch. The decision comes amid widespr...

item.title: Justin Stamper's Real Estate Empire and TV Success
item.description: Justin Stamper, an American real estate investor and television personality, rose to fame through his work on A&E's Zombie House Flipping. He started flipping properties at 19 after saving his family'...

item.title: Hamilton Bus Thief Sentenced to Nine Months for Mental Health Treatment and Probation
item.description: A 36-year-old man who stole a Hamilton city bus during a joy ride and left passengers on board has been sentenced to nine months in jail. The court recommended he receive mental health treatment at a ...
